# Argus

> Multi-provider search broker for AI agents. Routes queries across SearXNG, Brave, Serper, Tavily, and Exa with automatic fallback, RRF ranking, health tracking, and budget enforcement. Extract clean text from any URL. Multi-turn sessions with context-enriched follow-ups. API key auth. SQLite only — zero external database dependencies. Connect via HTTP, CLI, MCP, or Python.

## Installation

> pip install -e ".[mcp]" to install with MCP support. Configure provider API keys in .env (at least one of: Brave, Serper, Tavily, Exa). Run `argus serve` for HTTP API on port 8000, or `argus mcp serve` for MCP server over stdio.

## MCP Server

> Start with `argus mcp serve`. Tools: search_web, extract_content, recover_url, expand_links, search_health, search_budgets, test_provider. Add to Claude Code, Cursor, or VS Code via MCP config JSON. See README for copy-paste configs.

## HTTP API

> POST /api/search with {"query": "...", "mode": "discovery", "max_results": 10}. Set ARGUS_API_KEY to require authentication (health exempt). OpenAPI docs at /docs. Endpoints: /api/search, /api/extract, /api/recover-url, /api/expand, /api/health, /api/budgets.

## Search Modes

> discovery: find related pages and canonical sources. recovery: find a moved or dead URL. grounding: few live sources for fact-checking. research: broad exploratory retrieval.

## Python SDK

> from argus.broker.router import create_broker; from argus.models import SearchQuery, SearchMode; broker = create_broker(); response = await broker.search(SearchQuery(query="...", mode=SearchMode.DISCOVERY)); content = await extract_url(response.results[0].url)

## CLI

> argus search -q "query" --mode discovery; argus extract -u "https://example.com/article"; argus health; argus budgets; argus test-provider -p brave

## Architecture

> SQLite for all persistence (search history, budgets, sessions, extraction cache). WAL mode for reliability. Generic TTLCache and SlidingWindowLimiter in argus/core/. No SQLAlchemy, no PostgreSQL.

## Documentation

> README.md — full documentation with quick start, integration examples, configuration
> CHANGELOG.md — version history
> docs/providers.md — provider setup and budget configuration
> docs/routing-contract.md — authoritative routing, fallback, and ranking spec
> docs/search-modes.md — when to use each search mode, with examples
> docs/mcp-tools.md — MCP tool and resource reference with parameters and examples
> docs/troubleshooting.md — common issues and fixes
> CONTRIBUTING.md — contribution guide and adding providers
